<!-- auto-generated by scripts/build_docs.py — do not edit -->

*[RAG]: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: grounding LLM output in documents fetched at inference time.
*[Embedding]: A dense numeric vector representing the meaning of text so similar items sit close together.
*[Vector database]: A store optimized for similarity search over embedding vectors.
*[ANN]: Approximate Nearest Neighbor search: fast similarity lookup that trades exactness for speed.
*[Reranking]: A second-stage model that reorders retrieved candidates by relevance to the query.
*[Chunking]: Splitting documents into smaller passages before embedding and indexing.
*[Fine-tuning]: Continuing training of a pretrained model on task- or domain-specific data.
*[LoRA]: Low-Rank Adaptation: fine-tuning by training small added matrices instead of all weights.
*[QLoRA]: LoRA applied on top of a quantized base model to cut fine-tuning memory dramatically.
*[PEFT]: Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning: methods that update only a small fraction of model parameters.
*[RLHF]: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback: aligning a model using human preference signals.
*[DPO]: Direct Preference Optimization: preference alignment without a separate reward model.
*[Quantization]: Representing model weights/activations in fewer bits to reduce memory and speed inference.
*[KV cache]: Cached attention keys/values from prior tokens so each new token is cheap to generate.
*[PagedAttention]: A memory technique that pages the KV cache to boost LLM serving throughput.
*[Continuous batching]: Dynamically adding/removing requests from a running inference batch to maximize GPU use.
*[Throughput]: Work completed per unit time, e.g. tokens or requests per second.
*[Latency]: Time to complete a single request, end to end.
*[TTFT]: Time To First Token: latency from request until the first output token streams back.
*[Token]: The sub-word unit LLMs read and generate; billing and context limits are counted in tokens.
*[Context window]: The maximum number of tokens a model can attend to in one request.
*[Prompt caching]: Reusing computation for a repeated prompt prefix to cut cost and latency.
*[Agent]: An LLM-driven system that plans and takes actions via tools to accomplish a goal.
*[ReAct]: An agent pattern interleaving reasoning steps with tool actions.
*[Tool use]: Letting a model call external functions/APIs via structured outputs.
*[Orchestration]: A central coordinator explicitly directs each step of a workflow.
*[Choreography]: Services react to events independently with no central coordinator.
*[Saga]: A sequence of local transactions with compensating actions for distributed consistency.
*[Idempotency]: An operation that produces the same result no matter how many times it is applied.
*[Event-driven architecture]: A style where components communicate by producing and reacting to events.
*[Message broker]: Middleware that routes messages between producers and consumers.
*[Dead-letter queue]: A queue holding messages that could not be processed successfully.
*[Eventual consistency]: A model where replicas converge to the same state given enough time without updates.
*[Data mesh]: A decentralized approach treating data as a product owned by domain teams.
*[Data contract]: An enforced agreement on the schema and semantics of data between producer and consumer.
*[Feature store]: Infrastructure that stores and serves features consistently for training and inference.
*[Training-serving skew]: Discrepancy between features computed at training time and at serving time.
*[Data drift]: A shift in the input distribution P(X) over time.
*[Concept drift]: A shift in the relationship P(Y|X) so past patterns no longer hold.
*[MLOps]: Engineering practices for reliably building, deploying, and operating ML in production.
*[Model registry]: A versioned catalog of trained models and their lifecycle stage.
*[Experiment tracking]: Recording parameters, metrics, and artifacts across training runs for comparison.
*[Precision]: Of the items predicted positive, the fraction that are actually positive.
*[Recall]: Of the actual positives, the fraction the model correctly identified.
*[F1 score]: The harmonic mean of precision and recall.
*[ROC AUC]: Area under the ROC curve: probability a random positive ranks above a random negative.
*[Confusion matrix]: A table of true/false positives and negatives for a classifier.
*[Eval]: A structured test that scores a model's quality on representative inputs.
*[Hallucination]: Confident model output that is unsupported or factually wrong.
*[SOLID]: Five OO design principles: SRP, OCP, LSP, ISP, DIP.
*[Dependency inversion]: Depend on abstractions, not concrete implementations.
*[Liskov substitution]: Subtypes must be usable anywhere their base type is expected.
*[Design pattern]: A reusable, named solution to a recurring software design problem.
*[Singleton]: A pattern ensuring a class has exactly one shared instance.
*[Factory]: A pattern that creates objects without exposing concrete construction logic.
*[Strategy]: A pattern that makes interchangeable algorithms selectable at runtime.
